source: Invest Like the Best — The cyclone under the surface (EP.260)
summary: Semiconductor industry has structurally shifted to ~3x nominal GDP growth rate from ~2x, driven by AI and EV/AV content
summary_extended: Baker argues that the semiconductor industry has undergone a permanent structural shift in its growth rate relative to nominal GDP, moving from approximately 2x to approximately 3x nominal GDP growth. The primary drivers are two secular forces: first, AI training, which requires exponentially more compute as model quality scales with data (citing a Microsoft research paper showing quality doubles with every 10x increase in training data), meaning AI is effectively 'eating software' and displacing it with compute-intensive workloads; second, the electrification and eventual autonomy of vehicles, which dramatically increases semiconductor content per vehicle. Baker frames this as a long-term foundational thesis that justifies sustained bullishness on the semiconductor sector even through near-term cyclical downturns. The investment implication is broad sector-level overweight on semiconductors rather than a single-name call, as the thesis is about the industry's aggregate growth trajectory.
detail: AI training requires exponentially more compute (quality doubles with every 10x increase in training data per a Microsoft research paper). AI is eating software. EVs and eventually AVs dramatically increase semiconductor content per vehicle. These two forces have permanently shifted the semiconductor industry's growth multiplier relative to GDP upward.
confidence_evidence: Baker cites specific research (Microsoft paper on AI scaling), frames this as a structural long-term view, and uses it as the foundation for his long-term bullishness on semis post-cycle.
quote_fragment: used to grow at 2x nominal GDP to one that probably now is a 3x nominal GDP grower
